name: Integrity Student Trnsprtn
districts_or_customers: Atlantic County NJ area districts (Galloway Township/Egg Harbor City vicinity)
recent_news: Active operator; NJDEP Phase 2 VW Settlement proposal submitted for clean bus funding (recent years). Mixed online reviews; no public M&A signal.
fit_notes: Single-owner Atlantic County NJ school bus contractor with ~200 employees and ~$8M revenue; sits inside Summit's NJ footprint and is a classic tuck-in profile. Owner-operator under Joe Duncan suggests possible succession opportunity if owner is long-tenured.

name: Dufour Tours
recent_news: Discontinued motor coach/tour business after 37 years when co-owners William and Barbara Dufour decided to retire (per Berkshire Eagle); core school bus business continues across MA and VT.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: 60+ year family-owned (founded 1963 as Dufour Brothers in CT), multi-district MA/VT footprint, ~$7.6M revenue, ~200 staff. Owners' retirement of the tour line and CEO William Dufour's likely advanced age create a clear succession signal. Sits in Summit MA territory with adjacent VT expansion.

name: Chief School Bus Svc
recent_news: 47-year-old operator; long-running contract with Westside Community Schools. No public M&A signal. Also lists a related Chief School Bus Sales operation in Omaha.
fit_notes: Established 1979 with same President (Mose Turner) and VP (George Giboo) - strong succession signal given 45+ year tenure. Anchor Westside Community Schools contract is a stable, recurring revenue base. NE is an expansion market for Summit but the platform is small (~$7M, 75 employees) and tied to one major district - dependency risk to diligence.

name: Goldstar Transit
owner_or_parent: Student Transportation of America (STA) - parent owned by CDPQ
principals: Operated as STA subsidiary (DBA: Gold Star Transit, Inc.)
Tina Meads (local Manager per source data)
districts_or_customers: 10+ Texas districts across Fort Worth Metroplex and beyond
recent_news: STA subsidiary; January 2025 STA announced series of US acquisitions adding $70M+ revenue. GoldStar renewed/expanded Bastrop ISD contract. Not an acquisition target.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in candidate - owned by Student Transportation of America (STA), a major Summit competitor at the platform level. Flag as a competitive datapoint for Texas market intelligence rather than acquisition target.

name: MBG Enterprises Inc
recent_news: Active family operator (Gunther family, second generation in VP roles). No public M&A signal. Dual-line business (school bus + refuse) inspected by Maryland PSC and county schools.
fit_notes: Solid tuck-in: 77 buses serving two large MD districts (Howard, Anne Arundel) in Baltimore-Washington corridor; founded 1987 by Mitch Gunther with three family VPs (next-gen in seat). Medium succession signal given founder still active and family successors involved. Refuse line is a carve-out consideration in any deal structure.
